channel.title: Growth on Marketing Blogs
channel.description: Recent content in Growth on Marketing Blogs

item.title: SEO That Compounds: Why Most Teams Plateau at 18 Months
item.description:
Every B2B content team I have worked with hits the same wall somewhere between month 14 and month 22. Traffic rises steadily, then flattens. The instinct is to publish more, hire faster, or chase shinier formats. None of those usually move the line.

The teams that break through tend to share three habits. None of them are about volume.

They treat topics as portfolios, not articles

Most plans look like a list of post titles. The teams that compound look at a topic the way a fund manager looks at a sector. A “topic” might be ten or twenty individual URLs that all share intent and link laterally to one another. Each new piece raises the ceiling for the others. A single article in isolation almost never does.

item.title: The Attribution Conversation Nobody Wants to Have
item.description:
Two things are true at the same time, and most marketing teams refuse to hold them together.

  1. The CFO needs an answer about which channels are working.
  2. The multi-touch attribution dashboard is mostly theater.

The team’s job is to live with both facts without giving up.

What the dashboard actually shows

Most multi-touch attribution tools assign fractional credit to touchpoints across the journey using one of a handful of models: linear, time-decay, position-based, “data-driven.” All of these are reasonable as heuristics and none of them are causal.
